quests.title: Forge Your First Alire Crate
quests.summary: Create, build, and run a new Ada executable using Alire.
instructions: Create a new directory under your Ada mastery workspace.
Run alr init --bin ada_first_crate.
Build it with alr build.
Run it with alr run.
Change the program so it prints your name and the words Ada mastery begins.
acceptance: The crate contains alire.toml.
alr build succeeds.
alr run prints the required message.
quests.commands:
mkdir -p ~/Documents/src/projects/ada-mastery
cd ~/Documents/src/projects/ada-mastery
alr init --bin ada_first_crate
cd ada_first_crate
alr build
alr run

quests.title: Variable Before Use
quests.summary: Explain the rule that keeps an Ada variable from being read before it has a value.
quests.question: What must happen before an Ada variable can safely be read?
instructions: Answer in your own words. One sentence is enough.
acceptance: The answer explains that the object must receive a valid value before being read.

quests.title: Constant or Variable?
quests.summary: Read two declarations and explain which object may change.
quests.question: In `Max_Speed : constant Integer := 450;` and `Speed : Integer := 0;`, which object may be assigned a new value later, and why?
instructions: Name both objects and explain the difference.
acceptance: Speed may change; Max_Speed is constant after initialization.

quests.title: Range-Checked Altitude
quests.summary: Use a subtype to prevent an impossible aircraft altitude.
instructions: Create subtype Altitude_Feet with a range from 0 through 60_000.
Declare Current_Altitude using that subtype.
Print the altitude.
Temporarily assign 70_000 and observe the runtime range check.
acceptance: Altitude_Feet is a subtype with an explicit range.
The valid assignment runs.
The invalid assignment demonstrates Constraint_Error.

quests.title: Predict the Range Check
quests.summary: Predict what happens when a value violates a subtype range.
quests.question: Given `subtype Percent is Integer range 0 .. 100; P : Percent := 101;`, what happens and which exception is involved?
instructions: State whether this is accepted and name the exception.
acceptance: The answer identifies a failed range check and Constraint_Error.

quests.title: Parameter Mode: in
quests.summary: Explain what an `in` parameter communicates to callers.
quests.question: What does parameter mode `in` mean in an Ada procedure?
instructions: Explain both what the procedure can do and what it cannot do.
acceptance: The parameter supplies input and is not assigned by the procedure.

quests.title: Move the Aircraft
quests.summary: Write a procedure that updates an existing position.
instructions: Create a record containing X and Y coordinates.
Write Move with Position as `in out` and Delta_X/Delta_Y as `in`.
Call Move twice and print the final position.
acceptance: Only the position uses `in out`.
The delta parameters use `in` or the default mode.
The original record is updated.

quests.title: Repair the Parameter Mode
quests.summary: Fix a procedure that tries to modify an input-only parameter.
quests.question: What change is required so this procedure may update `Value`? `procedure Increment (Value : in Integer) is begin Value := Value + 1; end Increment;`
instructions: Name the corrected parameter mode and explain why.
acceptance: Value is changed from `in` to `in out`.

quests.title: Flight Mode Case Statement
quests.summary: Model a finite set of flight modes with exhaustive handling.
instructions: Declare Flight_Mode as an enumeration containing Parked, Taxi, Climb, Cruise, and Landing.
Write a case statement that prints a distinct message for every value.
Do not use `when others`.
acceptance: The enumeration represents only valid flight modes.
Every value is handled explicitly.
The compiler can detect a missing branch.

quests.title: Split a Package
quests.summary: Separate a public interface from its implementation.
instructions: Create aircraft_status.ads and aircraft_status.adb.
Expose Set_Altitude and Get_Altitude in the specification.
Keep the stored altitude private inside the body.
Use the package from the crate's main procedure.
acceptance: Clients depend on the specification.
The stored state is not directly visible to clients.
alr build and alr run succeed.

quests.title: Three-Sensor Snapshot
quests.summary: Represent several strongly typed sensor readings.
instructions: Create a Sensor_Reading record with Name, Value, and Healthy fields.
Create a fixed array containing exactly three readings.
Loop over the array and print each sensor's state.
acceptance: The related fields are grouped in a record.
The three records are held in an array.
Iteration does not use magic indexes outside the array range.

quests.title: Protect the Input Boundary
quests.summary: Handle invalid text at the input boundary instead of hiding every error.
instructions: Read an altitude as text.
Convert it with Integer'Value.
Handle Constraint_Error near the conversion.
Do not use `when others => null`.
acceptance: Invalid numeric text produces a useful message.
The exception handler is close to the risky conversion.
Unexpected exceptions are not silently discarded.

quests.title: GtkAda First Window
quests.summary: Create a minimal GtkAda window from an Alire crate.
instructions: Create a new Alire executable crate.
Add GtkAda as an Alire dependency using the crate version available on your system.
Create a window titled Ada Mastery.
Add a label and a Close button.
Connect the window destroy signal so the application exits cleanly.
acceptance: The dependency is recorded in alire.toml.
The window opens from alr run.
The button or window close control exits cleanly.
quests.commands:
alr init --bin gtkada_first_window
cd gtkada_first_window
alr with gtkada
alr build
alr run

quests.title: Foundation Capstone: Aircraft Status
quests.summary: Combine Alire, types, packages, records, input validation, and GtkAda.
instructions: Build an Alire-based GtkAda aircraft status application.
Keep aircraft state in a non-GUI package.
Display heading, altitude, airspeed, and health state.
Validate edited values with Ada ranges or subtypes.
Show a useful error dialog for invalid input.
Add at least one AUnit test for the non-GUI package.
acceptance: Domain logic builds without depending on GtkAda widgets.
The UI calls the domain package through a clear API.
Invalid values cannot silently enter the model.
The test can run independently of the GUI.
